title: salix × ampherista subsp. yamatensis
intro: Salix × ampherista subsp. yamatensis, also known as the Japanese willow, is a hybrid species of the Salix genus, specifically a cross between Salix sachalinensis and Salix caprea. This plant has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antiseptic properties. The health benefits of Salix × ampherista subsp. yamatensis include treating fever, rheumatism, and wounds, as well as providing relief from pain and inflammation. Herbal preparations of this plant include teas, infusions, and tinctures, which are often used in traditional Japanese medicine to promote overall health and well-being.
